      // ArrowDown/ArrowUp move focus between menu items (wrapping at the

      const itemsOf = (menu: HTMLElement): HTMLElement[] =>

      async function openFileMenu(): Promise<HTMLElement[]> {
        const { results } = await setupCleared();
        renderBrowse(browseResult({ path: 'docs', entries: [file] }));
        const actions = actionsOf(results, 'a.txt');
        menuBtnOf(actions).click();
        const menu = menuOf(actions);
        expect(menu.hidden).toBe(false);
        return itemsOf(menu);
      }

      /** Dispatch a menu-navigation key on the open menu's document listener. */
      function pressMenuKey(key: string): KeyboardEvent {
        const evt = new KeyboardEvent('keydown', { key, cancelable: true });
        document.dispatchEvent(evt);
        return evt;
      }

      it('ArrowDown moves focus from the first menu item to the second', async () => {
        const items = await openFileMenu();
        items[0].focus();
        expect(document.activeElement).toBe(items[0]);

        expect(document.activeElement).toBe(items[1]);
        expect((items[1].textContent ?? '').trim()).toBe('Delete');
        // Handled keys preventDefault so they do not scroll the page.
        expect(evt.defaultPrevented).toBe(true);
      });

      it('ArrowUp wraps focus from the first item back to the last', async () => {
        const items = await openFileMenu();
        items[0].focus();
        expect(document.activeElement).toBe(items[0]);

        const evt = pressMenuKey('ArrowUp');

        // Wraps past the first item (index 0) around to the last (Copy).
        expect(document.activeElement).toBe(items[items.length - 1]);
        expect((items[items.length - 1].textContent ?? '').trim()).toBe('Copy');
        expect(evt.defaultPrevented).toBe(true);
      });

      it('Home moves focus to the first menu item', async () => {

        pressMenuKey('ArrowDown');
        expect(document.activeElement).toBe(items[3]);

Title: Menu advertises role="menu" but still lacks arrow-key item navigation (Up/Down/Home/End)

Finding:
The prior menu-semantics finding (role="menu" advertised but not implemented) was PARTIALLY fixed: createMenu now sets role="menu" on the container, role="menuitem" on each child, and moves focus onto the first item on open (items[0]?.focus()). However, the WAI-ARIA Menu Button keyboard interaction pattern also requires: ArrowDown/ArrowUp to move focus between menu items (wrapping), Home/End to jump to first/last item, and focus staying within the menu while it is open. The only keydown handler registered (onDocKey) still handles Escape alone. Because role="menu" is now explicitly set, screen readers will announce the dropdown as a menu and users will naturally reach for the arrow keys; finding them inert is a confusing expectation mismatch. (Tab/Shift+Tab still moves between items and Escape closes, so the menu is functional — just not matching the advertised pattern.) This is the remaining gap in the previously-reported medium finding.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
In src/app/dom-builders.ts, the `createMenu` function builds a `role="menu"` dropdown whose `onDocKey` document listener currently only closes on Escape. Complete the WAI-ARIA Menu Button keyboard pattern so the menu matches its advertised semantics:

1. Inside `onDocKey` (or a new keydown listener attached to the menu element itself when it opens and removed on close), handle ArrowDown, ArrowUp, Home, and End while the menu is open:
   - ArrowDown: move focus to the next `role="menuitem"` child (wrapping from last to first).
   - ArrowUp: move focus to the previous `role="menuitem"` child (wrapping from first to last).
   - Home: move focus to the first item.
   - End: move focus to the last item.
   Determine the currently-focused item via `document.activeElement` and the item list via the `items` array already captured in `createMenu`. Guard so these keys are only handled when the menu is open (not hidden). Call `event.preventDefault()` for the handled arrow/Home/End keys so they do not scroll the page.
2. Keep the existing Escape handling (close the menu).
3. Add focused tests in src/app/render-browse.test.ts covering: ArrowDown moves focus from the first menu item to the second; ArrowUp wraps from the first item to the last; Home focuses the first item; End focuses the last item. Reference the existing 'opening the menu moves focus onto the first menu item' test for setup patterns (menuBtnOf/actionsOf/menuOf helpers, setupCleared, file/dir fixtures).

Do not change the menu's role attributes, focus-on-open behavior, or Escape handling — only add the arrow/Home/End key handling.
